semantic_text:
ref(insights): replace `span.module` with `span.category` everywhere (#92575)

To make it easier to add open in explore functionality, the queries
should be supported in explore too.
`span.category` is in explore, while `span.module` is not, In eap
`span.module` just maps to `span.category` anyways so there's no reason
to use

In this Pr we
1. Replace `span.module` with `span.category`
2. When `span.module = db` we add a `!span.op[db.redis,db.sql.room]`
filter. With `span.module` in the metrics dataset, the backend used to
map `db.redis` -> `cache` and `db.sql.room` -> `other`
- Updated the code to load the profile preview in the detail panel - Added tests

feat(trace-eap-waterfall): EAP transaction spans should represent trace (#92992) In the img below both the inp span and the eap span with `is_transaction:true` are roots. In such a scenario, we want the latter to represent the trace (i.e. used in the header and we use its attrs as the trace level attrs Co-authored-by: Abdullah Khan
feat(trace-eap-waterfall): Minor iteration
feat(source-maps): Introduce new empty state copies and react-native callout (#92286)
feat(source-maps): Do not show pagination together with empty state (#92287)

In the img below both the inp span and the eap span with `is_transaction:true` are roots. In such a scenario, we want the latter to represent the trace (i.e. used in the header and we use its attrs as the trace level attrs
feat(trace-eap-waterfall): Adding tests
- Stand alone spans don't have `transaction_id`s. There `isPending` is always true. - Using isLoading instead and only rendering the transaction specific sections if the data exists.
- Updated the code to load the profile preview in the detail panel - Added tests
- Stand alone spans don't have `transaction_id`s. There `isPending` is always true. - Using isLoading instead and only rendering the transaction specific sections if the data exists.

ref(insights): remove `span.module` filter if `span.op` is provided (#92578)

If there is an equivalent query that can be represented in a simpler
manor, it should be used instead. When you open an insight chart in
explore, we don't want users to be confused what the query is doing.

In this case we remove the `span.module` filter, anytime there's an
appropriate `span.op` filter as well.
for example `span.category:http span.op:http.client` - used in the http
module, can be simplified to `span.op:http.client` as the category
filter is a superset of the operation filter

fix(search-bar) Constrain width of list box (#91613)

This PR constrains the max-width of the value list box so that it
doesn't explode across the entire screen.

feat(trace-eap-waterfall): Adding support for sibling autogroup in eap traces (#92802) - Autogrouping `>= 5` sibling spans with the same op and description - Added tests. - Example demo org trace for testing: [link](https://demo.dev.getsentry.net:7999/explore/traces/trace/ec36300b1c6b478fa51bb5d2c50ab973/?mode=samples&node=span-a97f140573066e08&pageEnd&pageStart&project=-1&source=traces&statsPeriod=24h&tab=waterfall&table=trace×tamp=1748977852.977) --------- Co-authored-by: Abdullah Khan
chore(demomode): remove from codeowners (#92625)

feat(trace-eap-waterfall): Reparenting http.server spans under pageload and browser.request spans (#92913) - This behaviour is enabled for the current nodestore/transaction based trace waterfall. On load: On expansion: Co-authored-by: Abdullah Khan

ref(auto_config): Split FrameInfo into its own module (#92301)

The primary purpose of this change is to decouple the frame information
logic from the code mappings module.
ref(onboarding): centralize project flags & signals (#91921)

feat: Update suspension modal with all valid reasons, and suspension modal copy (#92564) This PR is a small set of copy changes / updates to the suspension modal and suspension admin workflow. Also removes an unnecessary function in the suspension modal that wasn't being used anywhere. Part 1 of https://linear.app/getsentry/issue/BIL-785/account-suspension-account-suspension-message-is-inaccurate ### Legal Boilerplate Look, I get it.
